ogTitle: Smiths Gully vs Smokeytown: Climate Safety

heading: Smiths Gully vs Smokeytown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smiths Gully, Victoria and Smokeytown, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smiths Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Smokeytown (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Smiths Gully or Smokeytown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smiths Gully has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Smokeytown scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smiths Gully scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Smokeytown scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smiths Gully or Smokeytown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smiths Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
